mission.title: vllm: Support MiniMax-01 model
mission.description: Add support for the MiniMax-01 family of models (MiniMax-Text-01 and MiniMax-VL-01) in vLLM. MiniMax-VL-01 is a multimodal vision-language model using the ViT-MLP-LLM architecture. Requested by 38+ users.

steps.title: Check current vLLM setup
steps.description:
Verify your vLLM version and configuration:
```bash
pip show vllm | grep Version
# or
python -c "import vllm; print(vllm.__version__)"
```
MiniMax-01 support requires a recent version of vLLM with the model architecture registered.

steps.title: Download the MiniMax-01 model
steps.description:
The MiniMax-01 models are available on HuggingFace:
- Text-only: [MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-Text-01](https://huggingface.co/MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-Text-01)
- Vision-Language: [MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-VL-01](https://huggingface.co/MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-VL-01)

The VL model uses a ViT-MLP-LLM framework common in multimodal large language models.

steps.title: Serve MiniMax-01 with vLLM
steps.description:
Start the vLLM server with the MiniMax-01 model:
```bash
# For text-only model
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-Text-01

# For vision-language model
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-VL-01
```
Note: These are large models and require significant GPU memory.

steps.title: Verify MiniMax-01 inference works
steps.description:
Test that MiniMax-01 model serving is working:
```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-Text-01", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello!"}], "max_tokens": 50}'
```
You should receive a valid chat completion response from the MiniMax-01 model.

resolution.summary: vLLM added support for the MiniMax-01 family of models including MiniMax-Text-01 and MiniMax-VL-01. The vision-language model uses the ViT-MLP-LLM framework with a custom image processor. This enables high-throughput serving of MiniMax models through vLLM's inference engine.

prerequisites.description: Python 3.8+, pip, a CUDA-capable GPU with sufficient VRAM, and a working vLLM installation (pip install vllm).
